one_liner: TSM +4.9% to $477 — three-day SOS sequence ($432→$455→$477) with volume drying up on each leg; Barclays raised to $625 and Q2 earnings July 16; this is the cleanest accumulate setup in the watchlist.

price_action.intraday_story: TSM opened at $455.61 (gap up from $455.10 close), reached $479.00 intraday, and closed at $477.57 — a strong close in the upper third of the range. The three-session recovery from the June 26 low of $419.19: Day 1 ($432.35, 18M vol) → Day 2 ($455.10, 14.9M vol) → Day 3 ($477.57, 12.9M vol). Volume declining on each successive up day — exactly the Wyckoff pattern of 'effort reducing, result maintained' indicating absorption rather than forced buying.

news_catalyst.headline: Barclays Raises TSM to $625; UBS Lifts Taiwan-Listed to T$3,400; Q2 Earnings July 16
news_catalyst.summary: Two major analyst upgrades hit TSMC simultaneously on June 30. Barclays raised to $625 from $470 (Overweight) — a 33% upside from current levels — citing improved revenue and pricing outlook. UBS separately lifted its Taiwan-listed target to T$3,400 from T$3,000 (Buy), projecting TSMC could raise advanced process prices 5–10% as early as Q1 2027, reflecting continued supply tightness. Morgan Stanley projects 40% YoY revenue growth in 2026. Q2 earnings are scheduled for July 16, with consensus at $3.80 EPS on ~$40B revenue (+33% YoY). With 17 analysts Buy and 0 Sell, TSMC has the cleanest analyst consensus in the semiconductor space.

technical.summary: TSM's three-day recovery pattern — $432 → $455 → $477 — with volume contracting on each leg (18M → 14.9M → 12.9M) is one of the cleanest Wyckoff Sign of Strength (SOS) sequences in the watchlist. Grade A fundamentals (0.89, highest in the watchlist), dual analyst upgrades with a $625 Barclays target, and 17/0 analyst consensus form an unusually strong convergence of technical and fundamental buy signals.

wyckoff_narrative:
The June 26 session (low $419.19, 18M volume, 1.35x) marked a potential Selling Climax or Spring. The secondary test on June 29 (low $431.09, 14.9M volume, 1.12x) held above the SC low with lower volume — textbook ST behavior. Today's push to $477 on 12.9M volume (0.97x) — the lowest volume of the three-day sequence — is a Last Point of Support breaking into markup. This is Phase C completing and Phase D beginning in Wyckoff terms.

The declining volume on advancing prices is the critical confirmation: it means supply is being absorbed, not replaced. Each day the stock requires fewer shares to move higher, indicating sellers are running out of stock to sell at these prices. The $453 low today becomes the LPS — if TSMC pulls back and holds above $453 with below-average volume in the next 2 sessions, that's the re-entry point before the July 16 earnings catalyst.

valuation.summary: The DCF base case reflects an enormous ADR-adjusted margin of safety. On conventional metrics, Barclays' $625 target implies 31% upside. Grade A fundamentals (0.89 score) with 40% projected YoY revenue growth support the premium. The only persistent discount factor is Taiwan geopolitical risk, which is structural and won't change.

key_strengths: Highest fundamental score in the watchlist (0.89)
Barclays $625 target — 31% upside from current price
17 Buy / 0 Sell analyst consensus — cleanest in semiconductor space
Potential 5–10% advanced process price increases in Q1 2027 (UBS)
key_concerns: Taiwan geopolitical risk — permanent structural discount
Q2 earnings July 16 — consensus at $3.80 EPS on $40B revenue; any miss would break the technical pattern
Intel Foundry winning Google TPU orders nibbles at market share optionality
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals, three-day SOS sequence, dual analyst upgrades, 0 Sell ratings — TSM is the highest-conviction setup in today's watchlist. The only risk is binary: Taiwan geopolitics or a July 16 earnings miss.

risks: July 16 Q2 earnings: consensus $3.80 EPS / $40B revenue — the bar is high after two upgrades; a miss breaks the technical recovery
Taiwan geopolitical escalation — any military posturing causes immediate 10–15% ADR selloffs
$479 intraday resistance — failed to close above $479, needs to clear this level convincingly
Volume declining on advance is constructive but means any bad news sees a rapid reversal with no buyers below $453
